observations: v4b.md:3-10 opens with clipped interrogation pressure; the short-answer rhythm suits Jaina's exhaustion and Sylvanas's command style.
v4b.md:21-45 mixes Jaina's longer reasoning chains with blunt corrections, matching the style guide's introspection-vs-emphasis pattern.
v4b.md:153-163 now turns faster after the release order; changing 'fully absorbed' to 'caught up to it' removed a process-heavy stumble from the motion beat.
v4b.md:319-371 keeps peak-action sentences mostly under 9 words, with a few longer reorientation lines so the wolf/deer fight does not go monotone.
Measured rhythm stayed compliant after edits: average sentence length 9.11 words and roughly 43% of sentences at 6 words or fewer, which fits the guide's baseline snap.

fixes.rule: sentenceRhythmStandards.default — process-heavy phrasing dulled a fast transition beat
fixes.before: The room changed around the command before Jaina had fully absorbed it.
fixes.after: The room changed around the command before Jaina had caught up to it.

observations: v4b.md:17-18 stays concrete and field-usable: contaminated leylines, northward movement, ward-stones taking damage.
v4b.md:41-45 preserves Jaina's one deliberate over-technical slip, then corrects it in-scene; that beat remains character work rather than contamination.
v4b.md:211 now reads as Jaina's irritated field judgment instead of comparative narrator summary.
v4b.md:241 now swaps 'big convergence point' for 'first place it knots tight,' which keeps the information but removes briefing-room diction from speech.
I checked for planning/document voice flags from the anti-slop list; remaining technical terms are world-native magic vocabulary, not bureaucratic or modern-scientific leakage.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.avoid — explanatory comparative framing drifted toward document voice
fixes.before: Useful, and obvious mainly because it contrasted so sharply with the way mages rode when they thought a staff excused them from watching the hedges.
fixes.after: Useful, and obvious beside the way mages rode when they thought a staff excused them from watching the hedges.

fixes.rule: vocabularyStandards.baseline — spoken line sounded too report-like for field dialogue
fixes.before: Millhaven is the first big convergence point on the route I tracked.
fixes.after: Millhaven is the first place it knots tight on the route I tracked.

observations: Image density stayed low: roughly 0.63 marked images per thousand words in this pass, well under the guide's cap.
v4b.md:27 uses 'Like a struck string answering a note' as a clean magical comparison rooted in craft and sound, not ornament.
v4b.md:177 uses 'rot under varnish' once to sharpen contamination without stacking a second image on top of it.
v4b.md:223 uses 'stain through linen' to clarify spread through land and water; the image is concrete and immediately legible.
I checked for mixed domains, epic-fantasy abstractions, and decorative body metaphors; none required intervention.
Paragraph cadence stays inside spec: average paragraph length 20.27 words, with no paragraph over the guide's soft maximum.
v4b.md:171-205 moves cleanly from ward release to road exposure by consequence rather than clock-time scaffolding.
v4b.md:239-275 preserves the scene's midpoint correction: Jaina misreads Sylvanas, gets checked, then restates in her real register.
v4b.md:451-473 closes on a compact approach sequence and leaves chapter3 the village-entry beat, matching the handoff contract.
I checked transitions for banned planner language and summary bridges; the chapter remains causal rather than schematic.

fixes.rule: sceneTransitionConventions.avoid — editorial self-summary over-explained the correction beat

observations: Measured dialogue-to-narration ratio is 23:77. That sits below the chapter-wide 45:55 target, but this chapter's travel dread and action blocks justify a documented exception rather than added dialogue.
Attribution is compliant: 42 no-frills tags ('said'/'asked') against 3 stronger tags, keeping the scene far inside the style guide's cap on creative tags.
v4b.md:39-60 handles the interrogation as pressure dialogue with short follow-up narration, which matches competence-collision scene needs.
v4b.md:317-389 appropriately drops dialogue density during the attack, then restores spoken pressure as soon as bodies have room to speak.
Action beats mostly land after dialogue or as hinges into the next line; I did not find any mid-line beat clutter severe enough to rewrite.
v4b.md:11-31 keeps Jaina's attention on magical pressure, confiscated notes, and whether anyone is finally listening, which matches her perception priorities.
v4b.md:81-99 shows her thought pattern correctly: observation -> institutional obstacle -> conclusion about delay and consequence.
v4b.md:259-273 is the chapter's clearest humility check; trimming the narration around it keeps the correction in Jaina's voice instead of an editor's gloss.
v4b.md:305-307 keeps Antonidas as confirmation and witness, not sentimental rescue, which fits her intellect-first emotional register.
Sylvanas's spoken voice at v4b.md:133-167, 243-277, and 427-437 remains clipped, dry, and rank-secure; her questions still function as tests or orders.

fixes.before: Inadequate. She had come into this country ready to fight condescension and had brought her own with her.
fixes.after: Inadequate. She had come into this country ready to fight condescension and had brought her own.

fixes.rule: perPOVCalibration.expositionFilter — aftermath reflection leaned abstract after a physical diagnostic beat
fixes.before: Silence followed. Not blank silence. The kind that meant each of them had already started fitting the answer to her own work.
fixes.after: Silence followed. Not blank silence. The kind that meant each of them was already measuring what the answer changed.

observations: Adverbs per page measured 1.55 after edits, comfortably under the hard cap of 4.
Rough passive-voice rate measured 1.14%, comfortably under the hard cap of 4%.
Show-vs-tell remains within floor expectations; key conclusions are earned through ward pressure, road evidence, and carcass reading before Jaina states them.
Filter-word sweep found only light, functional use; none of the chapter's sensory beats were leaning on repeated 'she noticed/she felt' scaffolding.
No change was needed here because the hard metrics already passed and the line-level edits did not degrade them.
Exposition integration stays organic: magical explanation arrives because Sylvanas demands usable answers, not through narrator aside.
Power dynamics track correctly through sentence shape: Sylvanas gets shorter commands and clipped interruptions; Jaina lengthens when defending or explaining.
Scene shape is compliant across the chapter: hard-friction opening, road-read midpoint correction, infected-animal escalation, and an uneasy forward-pressure resolution at Millhaven.
Speculative elements are domesticated properly. Wards, leylines, and necromantic contamination read as working realities with tactical consequence, not spectacle.
Humor stays within policy: the dry beats at v4b.md:135-145 and 407-409 sharpen pressure without deflating horror, and the chapter lets humor thin as the plague evidence worsens.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — exact magical language stays readable and scene-bound; only one intentional early over-technical slip survives.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — longer reasoning chains puncture into blunt answers and fragments under pressure, consistent with her calibration.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — low and controlled; images stay grounded in craft, pressure, and contamination.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — feeling leaks through humiliation, urgency, and bodily strain rather than open labeling.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — she repeatedly moves from observation to pattern to hypothesis to correction.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — ward pressure, current, wrongness in air and ground, and bodily magical strain dominate her lane.
fingerprintChecks.humorType: pass — dry lines arrive as stress relief or irritation, never as quippy performance.
fingerprintChecks.powerPosture: pass — when blocked, she speeds up and over-explains; when she regains footing, the syntax clears.
fingerprintChecks.speculativeComfort: pass — magic is native craft to her, rendered as structure, pressure, and failure mode rather than awe.
fingerprintChecks.expositionFilter: pass — explanation stays tied to immediate consequence, with only minor abstractions tightened in this pass.

ruleViolations: v4b.md:211 had narrator-summary scaffolding around Jaina's read of ranger formation; trimmed.
v4b.md:241 originally used 'big convergence point' in spoken dialogue; replaced with more field-native phrasing.
v4b.md:267 over-explained Jaina's self-correction after Sylvanas checks her register; compressed.
v4b.md:275 editorial self-verdict after the correction beat was removed to keep the moment in-character.
